question: An operator opens the Operations panel on the Operate Processes view and the panel shows: "No operations have been created yet." What does this message indicate?

options.text: The Zeebe exporter is misconfigured and is dropping operation events.
No operations such as canceling, retrying, migrating, or modifying process instances have been started yet for this cluster, so there is nothing for the panel to display.
The user is missing the operate:read role and operations are hidden until it is granted.
Operate is paused and operations cannot be listed until it is resumed from Console.

a.text: The docs treat this message as an empty-state, not an error; it is not described as an exporter or configuration problem.

b.text: The docs include this exact note: if you don't have any operations the operations panel will show "No operations have been created yet." It is the empty-state for a cluster on which no operations have been performed.

c.text: The docs do not link this message to a missing role; it is shown whenever no operations exist.

d.text: There is no pause-state concept tied to this message in the docs; it simply reflects the absence of operations.

explanation: Operate uses this message as the empty-state for the Operations panel. Cancel, retry, migration, and modification all create operations that show up here; until at least one operation has been started, the panel reports that none have been created yet.
